title: Seven Hells - Lucky Girl Route
lead: Gym Rerun route (money farm) with the Seven Hells team + Meloetta. Order: Hoenn -> Kanto -> Sinnoh -> Unova -> Johto, gym by gym. Meloetta starts with Choice Specs. Heal when changing region. Goal: 3 turns per gym.
groupPrompt: Choose the region and the gym.

legend.meaning: 2 Choice Scarf, 1 Choice Specs, 1 Choice Band, 1 Wide Lens. So Meloetta swaps item: starts with Specs (Hoenn), becomes Scarf in Kanto, and goes back to Specs after Kanto (and never swaps again).
Each Pokémon does ONE action per turn. 'A + B' = the 1st lead does A and the 2nd lead does B, in the SAME turn.
Turn number. In a multi-turn solve each line is a turn — and both leads act in ALL of them.
Using an X-Item spends that Pokemon's action on the turn; the other lead still attacks. It appears in the color of WHO uses it (not the target).
Swap A for B. The mon that was NOT swapped still attacks normally. 'A » B » C' = swap A»B this turn and B»C the next.
Each move appears in the color of the Pokemon that uses it in that gym. Moves used by more than one team member change color depending on who attacks.
Each gym in 3 turns (3-turn solve). Mold Breaker Jolly on Excadrill is mandatory.
Skips the gym and goes straight to the next stop on the route.

h_rustboro_vs_tyranitar_probopass_tyrani.title: Rustboro · vs. Tyranitar + Probopass / Tyranitar + Relicanth

steps.text: Cloud Nine: don't worry about the weather, just spam {Blizzard|Vanilluxe}.
Let the Aero/Typh die and send in Vanilluxe.
Optional. Requires Aero or Typh with the egg move Foresight. Use Repel for a smooth transition (here and in Kanto).
Corsola on T2: another X.SpA on Meloetta (or {Helping Hand|Typhlosion} if Typhlosion is on the field).

k_vermillion_chuva_ou_electrode_no_campo.title: Vermillion · Rain or Electrode on the field

steps.text: Heal upon entering Kanto. Switch Meloetta to Choice Scarf (it stays on Scarf throughout ALL of Kanto).

options.label: Rain or Electrode on the field

steps.text: T1: swap Aero » Vanilluxe (the Blastoise uses Water Spout). Then {Blizzard|Vanilluxe} + Water Spout.
Before Saffron: heal the Aero if it isn't at full HP.
Let Aero die and bring in Vanilluxe.

k_fuschia_meloetta_entrou_por_red_card_g.title: Fuschia · Meloetta entered via Red Card + Gengar on the field

steps.text: Shadow Ball / Psychic on Gengar + Eruption.
If Excadrill comes in via Red Card, it uses {Rock Slide|Excadrill}. Watch out: the enemy Dugtrio ('Gopher') uses Earthquake and kills Typhlosion.

options.label: Meloetta came in via Red Card + Gengar on the field

steps.text: If it rains: bring in Vanilluxe when Aero goes down.
If it rains: bring in Vanilluxe when Aero goes down.
Heal upon entering Sinnoh. Put Meloetta back to Choice Specs — and do NOT change her item again.
Aero uses Sunny Day (then {Rock Slide|Aerodactyl}) + Eruption.
Solve on Melo + Typh to ensure the Milotic variation dies in 3 turns.
Protect (Aero) + Earthquake. The Exca needs high Def IVs so it doesn't attract the Earthquake.
Small chance to survive with {Rock Slide|Aerodactyl} + Earthquake — 3-turn gamble, needs 31 HP and 31 Def.
After this gym, refill the PP of Water Spout and {Blizzard|Vanilluxe} with Leppa Berries.
After this gym, refill the PP of Water Spout and {Blizzard|Vanilluxe} with Leppa Berries.

u_driftveil_vs_sableye_ou_seismitoad_no_.title: Driftveil · vs. Sableye (or Seismitoad on T2)

steps.text: After this gym, refill the PP of Water Spout and {Blizzard|Vanilluxe} with Leppa Berries.
Aero uses Sunny Day (then {Rock Slide|Aerodactyl}) + Eruption.

u_mistralton_com_whimsicott_ou_skarmory_.title: Mistralton · With Whimsicott or Skarmory in the lead

steps.text: Use double {Blizzard|Blastoise} whenever possible, or refill PP with Leppa to ensure the 3-turn solve.
If you did Driftveil, you can just spam {Blizzard|Blastoise} + Water Spout.

options.label: With Whimsicott or Skarmory on the lead

steps.text: Last of Unova — can be reckless: with Blaze (low HP) Typhlosion can still gigaspam A.
Last of Unova — can be reckless: with Blaze (low HP) Typhlosion can still gigaspam A.
Switch both to Aero + Exca ({Rock Slide|Aerodactyl} + Earthquake).
Aero uses Sunny Day (then {Rock Slide|Aerodactyl}) + Eruption.
T1: Hyper Voice + {X.SpA|Typhlosion} (guarantees OHKO on all variations).
T2+: Hyper Voice + {Helping Hand|Typhlosion}, if Donphan/Rhyperior are not on the field.
Optional extra gym — need to heal first.
Optional extra gym — need to heal first.
Then: let the Aero die and bring in Vanilluxe.
Skipped in the doc. Legacy solve — mentions Togekiss, which this team no longer uses.
Doc: Togekiss + Typhlosion. If Chansey/Blissey on the field: switch Typhlosion » Excadrill.
Reason to skip: Meloetta does not kill Blissey in one hit and Typhlosion does not kill Slaking in one hit.
Switch both leads to Vanilluxe + Blastoise.

x_canalave_skarmory_ou_moltres_no_campo.title: Canalave (skipped) · Skarmory or Moltres on the field

steps.text: Switch both leads to Vanilluxe + Blastoise.
Skipped in the doc: terrible RNG + Flash Fire, mons 71. Include only if you scout beforehand.

options.label: Skarmory or Moltres on the field

steps.text: {Rock Slide|Aerodactyl} missed (Aero): log out and wait for the cooldown.
Your mon died: send another — use your head to decide who.
Physically tanky (Tangrowth/Exeggutor): keep pushing, it becomes 4+ turns.
Especially tanky (Chansey/Blissey/Snorlax/Milotic/Walrein/Tyranitar): X-Items or switch the lead to physical (Aero/Exca).
Fast (Electrode / Lucario Scarf @ Mossdeep): X.Speed on your sweeper or X.Atk on Aero.
Sand Rush (enemy Excadrill): two ways to play.
Swift Swim (Floatzel/Kabutops): log out and wait for the cooldown.
Chlorophyll (Lilligant/Venusaur): X.Spe; use the sun to kill Ninetales.
Slush Rush / Snow Cloak (Beartic/Froslass): Aero Sunny Day; Exca Mold Breaker.
Sun (Torkoal/Ninetales): X.Spe; use the sun to kill the Ninetales.
Rain (Pelipper/Politoed): switch to Vanilluxe (changes the weather immediately) or Aero Sunny Day.
Flash Fire (Typhlosion/Ninetales/Chandelure/Houndoom/Heatran): Aero Rain Dance; don't use fire attacks.
Water Absorb (Lanturn/Toxicroak/Cradily/Lapras/Seismitoad/Poliwrath/Vaporeon): log out/wait CD; don't use water attacks.
Scizor on the field: let Aero die or use Aero Protect.
Furret with Protect: open point ('Devs y').
Always heal when changing region. You can use Teleport Ocarina to optimize.
Heal Aero to full HP with potions of 20. Typh, Blast and Exca also recommended.
Burned physical attackers (Aero/Exca) only need to heal if they are used in the next gyms of the region.
Checkpoint bypass: if a mon died but won't be used until the next checkpoint (region change), you don't need to heal.
Items (1 stock): 2 Choice Scarf, 1 Choice Specs, 1 Choice Band, 1 Wide Lens. Only Meloetta swaps item (Specs <-> Scarf).
Teleport Ocarina and Fly Ocarina are mandatory. PP Max on the main moves. Main move in the 1st slot for gigaspam A.
Multi-device: offset the battles — start talking to the leader on the 2nd device only after the first 2 mons of the 1st fall.
